---
title: 'Unplanned Event Leads to Mindset Change: Seed Lifestyle'
date: '2014-11-23T18:08:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '8438841542284277322'
blogger_filename: '/2014/11/unplanned-event-leads-to-mindset-change.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Keys To Success'
  - 'Spiritual Growth'
---

<div class="WordSection1"><table align="center" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="tr-caption-container" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto; text-align: center;"><tbody><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/jd-hancock-lonely-traveler-toy-man-with-briefcase-on-counter-d3e48e61f073.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"><img alt="JD Hancock Lonely Traveler - Toy man with briefcase on counter. " border="0" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/jd-hancock-lonely-traveler-toy-man-with-briefcase-on-counter-d3e48e61f073.jpg" height="212" title="JD Hancock Lonely Traveler - Toy man with briefcase on counter. " width="320" /></a></td></tr><tr><td class="tr-caption" style="text-align: center;">Attribution: <a href="https://flic.kr/p/7AAuiL" target="_blank">JD Hancock</a>&nbsp; - Lonely Traveler </td></tr></tbody></table><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><h4 class="MsoNormal"><span style="font-family: &quot;Georgia&quot;,&quot;serif&quot;; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p>An Unplanned Passenger</o:p></span></h4><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal"><span style="font-family: &quot;Georgia&quot;,&quot;serif&quot;; font-size: 12.0pt;">When I passed the car, and then a block down the freeway I see a man in a grey suit pants and brown suit jacket with a brief case walking on the freeway. I heard inside of me,   â€œpick him upâ€. I obeyed immediately. I moved stuff aside and opened my door and leaned out and motioned my thumb to him â€œinternational hitch hiking symbolâ€.  <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal"><span style="font-family: &quot;Georgia&quot;,&quot;serif&quot;; font-size: 12.0pt;">Picked up Achoo (name changed for privacy) on the side of the road. Heâ€™s from NJ, and he just moved to Texas. He stated that his gas ran out, he thinks, and that was his car I had just passed. He was afraid to attempt to hold out his thumb to hitch   hike because he wasnâ€™t sure if it was illegal to do so in Texas.&nbsp;</span></div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal"><span style="font-family: &quot;Georgia&quot;,&quot;serif&quot;; font-size: 12.0pt;">Heâ€™s working for a tech company nearby, and he's working lots of OT because heâ€™s running low on money. Thatâ€™s why heâ€™s out of gas. Heâ€™s running late to work, and my picking him up is really helping him get there faster, maybe   an hour or more faster than walking. Heâ€™s very grateful, it was cold out there (30-40*F) and his jacket is light.  <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal"><span style="font-family: &quot;Georgia&quot;,&quot;serif&quot;; font-size: 12.0pt;">I asked if he had people at work that could help him back to deal with the car after work. He said he did. That part would be covered.&nbsp;  I drove him to work nearby and only ended up at work just a few moments later than I would have been.&nbsp;</span></div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal"><span style="font-family: &quot;Georgia&quot;,&quot;serif&quot;; font-size: 12.0pt;">I felt/heard God say â€œThank you, heâ€™s one of mineâ€.&nbsp;</span></div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal"><span style="font-family: &quot;Georgia&quot;,&quot;serif&quot;; font-size: 12.0pt;">I said â€œYouâ€™re welcomeâ€. I felt bad that I didnâ€™t   just get gas for his car.&nbsp;</span></div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><blockquote class="tr_bq"><div class="MsoNormal"><span style="font-family: &quot;Georgia&quot;,&quot;serif&quot;; font-size: 12.0pt;">God said â€œI have people for that. You are one cog in that wheel. You did good son.â€&nbsp;  God went on to say â€œIf you keep opening your heart to me, and spending time practicing to hear my voice, and open your heart to worship, youâ€™ll notice more and more of these unique moments   in life. Itâ€™s important to know about Favor, believe in Favor, be ready and willing to declare Favor over your life. BUTâ€¦ Favor is not a magic spell. Itâ€™s not something you can declare   every morning, without being in relationship with Me, and expect it to work the Favor Magic in your life. Favor is a byproduct of relationship with me. If you are deepening your relationship with me, Favor will come. Listen to Me. Hear Me. Obey what you hear.   Favor is just as likely to use you to bless someone as it is to use someone to bless you.â€  <o:p></o:p></span></div></blockquote><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal"><span style="font-family: &quot;Georgia&quot;,&quot;serif&quot;; font-size: 12.0pt;">Inside of me I saw/heard an inner me jump up and down and say â€œWhooooo Hoooo!â€  <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal"><h4>A little oil goes a long way! </h4><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal">So... this helped me shift my mindset a bit. Unintentionally, I fell into a rut of thinking that said I won't be able to really make a difference in people's lives until I reach higher levels of prosperity... so I've focused on what I could do to live out my calling and get prosperous. I mean... it's hard to make a difference when you are struggling to pay the house note, right?<br /><br />Well, the fact is that this is a noble aspiration, and a complete LIE! You have to sow seed before you get fruit. When you don't have much, you still do the same thing you do when you do have much. You seek God as your source, and you keep your heart and mind open to be used by God at a moments notice. You keep your attitude humble and open, and be aware of your surroundings. <br /><br />If you are a widow with one son, in a three year drought, and you only have a <a href="https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=2+Kings+4" target="_blank">little oil and flour</a>... then a man comes along and asks you to bake a cake for him... you can decide to help the guy, and risk giving from what little you have, or keep what little you have for yourself. In God's economy the first option produces results, if it's Spirit Led. </div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal">So, this week I sowed some oil, (which was turned into refined gasoline which I used to take a man to work, just a little out of my way)...<br /><br />I'm not concerned with my "reward" or "return on investment". I'm going to believe that God has my back, and there will be something coming. But I'm going to keep my focus on what I can do next. It was exciting. I'm ready for the next opportunity to do something for someone... what opportunity will be next? </div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal">Keep your eyes and ears open this week for opportunities to get our of your shell and help someone God brings across your path.</div><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq">In other words... will I #<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/2014/10/be-still-and-be-led-by-his-still-small.html" target="_blank">BeStillBeLed</a>? </blockquote><br /><br />---<br /><h4>And now I leave you with a question:</h4><blockquote class="tr_bq"><b>What do you think... when has God used YOU to help someone?</b></blockquote><br /><span style="font-family: Georgia, Times New Roman, serif;">To Respond Leave A Comment on This Post</span><br /><br /><h4><div><script src="https://apis.google.com/js/plusone.js"></script><g:plus action="share"></g:plus></div></h4><h4>By&nbsp;<a href="https://plus.google.com/+DarrellWolfe/posts" target="_blank">Darrell Wolfe</a></h4><div><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/about-20.html" style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif;" target="_blank">About This Blog, and Darrell.&nbsp;</a><br /><br /></div><div><h4><br /><span style="font-weight: bold;">This Weeks Resource:</span></h4><div><ul><li><a href="http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/0883687305/ref=as_li_tl?ie=UTF8&amp;camp=1789&amp;creative=390957&amp;creativeASIN=0883687305&amp;linkCode=as2&amp;tag=your0f4d-20&amp;linkId=3BFJVIAX35BOFKTJ">Grace: The Power To Change, Dr Jim Richards</a><img alt="" border="0" src="http://ir-na.amazon-adsystem.com/e/ir?t=your0f4d-20&amp;l=as2&amp;o=1&amp;a=0883687305" height="1" style="border: none !important; margin: 0px !important;" width="1" /> </li></ul><br /><iframe frameborder="0" marginheight="0" marginwidth="0" scrolling="no" src="//ws-na.amazon-adsystem.com/widgets/q?ServiceVersion=20070822&amp;OneJS=1&amp;Operation=GetAdHtml&amp;MarketPlace=US&amp;source=ss&amp;ref=ss_til&amp;ad_type=product_link&amp;tracking_id=your0f4d-20&amp;marketplace=amazon&amp;region=US&amp;placement=0883687305&amp;asins=0883687305&amp;linkId=ELKK4KX533GMNN52&amp;show_border=true&amp;link_opens_in_new_window=true" style="height: 240px; width: 120px;"></iframe><br /><br /><span style="font-family: Georgia, Times New Roman, serif;"><i><span style="line-height: 16px;">Affiliate Disclosure:</span></i></span><br /><span style="font-family: Georgia, Times New Roman, serif;"><i><span style="line-height: 16px;">I earn&nbsp;commissions&nbsp;off of some of my suggestions.&nbsp;</span></i><i style="line-height: 16px;">Full Disclaimers (<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/about-20.html" target="_blank">Click Here</a>)</i><br /><i>See my Store for more suggestions (<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/books-resources.html" rel="nofollow" target="_blank">Click Here</a>)</i></span></div><div>- <br /><br /><!-- Begin MailChimp Signup Form --><link href="//cdn-images.mailchimp.com/embedcode/slim-081711.css" rel="stylesheet" type="text/css"></link><style type="text/css"> #mc_embed_signup{background:#fff; clear:left; font:14px Helvetica,Arial,sans-serif;  width:200px;}  /* Add your own MailChimp form style overrides in your site stylesheet or in this style block.     We recommend moving this block and the preceding CSS link to the HEAD of your HTML file. */ </style><br /><div id="mc_embed_signup"><form action="//darrellwolfe.us3.list-manage.com/subscribe/post?u=979cb0db6ca74343e82a059ba&amp;id=14df3ba97b" class="validate" id="mc-embedded-subscribe-form" method="post" name="mc-embedded-subscribe-form" novalidate="" target="_blank"><label for="mce-EMAIL">Subscribe By Email</label> <input class="email" id="mce-EMAIL" name="EMAIL" placeholder="email address" required="" type="email" /> <!-- real people should not fill this in and expect good things - do not remove this or risk form bot signups--><br /><div style="left: -5000px; position: absolute;"><input name="b_979cb0db6ca74343e82a059ba_14df3ba97b" tabindex="-1" /></div><div class="clear"><input class="button" id="mc-embedded-subscribe" name="subscribe" type="submit" value="Subscribe" /></div></form></div><!--End mc_embed_signup--><br /><br /><br /></div></div><br /><br /></div>
